description: Self-improving error pattern database. Agents should append new entries when encountering novel error patterns.

categories.leakage: Any form of data leakage detection
categories.gate: Gate script execution and validation errors

entries.symptom: Gate script reports wrong error code when JSON parse fails for feature engineering report
entries.root_cause: except block used feature_group_spec_missing_or_invalid instead of feature_engineering_report_invalid
entries.fix: Change error code to feature_engineering_report_invalid in the except block
entries.prevention: Always match error codes to the specific artifact being parsed, not a related artifact

entries.symptom: transport_drop_ci shows [0.0, 0.0] CI bounds implying bootstrapped CI when none was computed
entries.root_cause: Hard-coded [0.0, 0.0] for ci_95 and 0.0 for ci_width in transport drop block
entries.fix: Replace with null and add ci_note: not_computed_point_estimate_only
entries.prevention: Never hard-code placeholder CI values; use null with explicit note when CI is not computed

entries.symptom: to_float() accepts inf and nan as valid float values, causing downstream gates to process non-finite metrics
entries.root_cause: Missing math.isfinite guard after float conversion
entries.fix: Add math.isfinite(v) check after float() conversion; return None if not finite
entries.prevention: All to_float implementations must include isfinite guard. Template: if not math.isfinite(v): return None

entries.symptom: Gate passes in strict mode despite having warnings, because finish() only checks failures
entries.root_cause: should_fail = bool(failures) ignores warnings in strict mode
entries.fix: Change to: should_fail = bool(failures) or (args.strict and bool(warnings))
entries.prevention: All gate finish() must use the pattern: bool(failures) or (args.strict and bool(warnings))

entries.symptom: Feature stability frequency analysis uses same random_state across all repeats
entries.root_cause: random_state=seed in loop does not change with repeat_idx
entries.fix: Use random_state=seed + repeat_idx to vary state per repeat
entries.prevention: In any loop performing repeated random sampling, ensure random_state varies with iteration index

entries.symptom: Model selection fails with candidate_pool_too_small when fewer than 3 models succeed
entries.root_cause: Insufficient model families selected, or optional backends not installed
entries.fix: Increase model families in --model-pool, raise --max-trials-per-family, or install optional backends (xgboost, catboost)
entries.prevention: For small datasets, use at least balanced preset (10 families). Check env_doctor for backend availability.

entries.symptom: ValueError: Cannot convert NaN to integer when assigning NaN to integer-typed numpy array
entries.root_cause: Attempting to assign np.nan to an integer-dtype column or array
entries.fix: Use DataFrame.loc[mask, col] = np.nan which auto-promotes dtype, or cast column to float first
entries.prevention: Before assigning NaN, ensure target column is float-compatible. Use pd.array with nullable integer dtype if needed.

entries.symptom: Training exceeds 20 minutes and times out with large dataset + many model candidates
entries.root_cause: Large dataset (>5000 rows) combined with comprehensive model pool and full bootstrap
entries.fix: Reduce model families, lower --max-trials-per-family, use conservative preset, or set --n-jobs to utilize multi-core
entries.prevention: For datasets >5000 rows: use adaptive bootstrap (auto-enabled), limit to balanced preset, set --n-jobs min(4, cpu_count)

entries.symptom: Warning: significant prevalence shift between train/valid/test splits
entries.root_cause: Random or temporal splitting created unbalanced prevalence across splits
entries.fix: Use stratified splitting (--strategy stratified_grouped) or verify temporal split preserves approximate prevalence
entries.prevention: Always check event rates per split after splitting. Use split_data.py which auto-warns on prevalence shift.

entries.symptom: Execution attestation gate fails because key files do not exist
entries.root_cause: Keypair not generated before running attestation gate
entries.fix: Run generate_execution_attestation.py which auto-bootstraps keypair if missing, or manually create with openssl
entries.prevention: Use mlgg onboarding flow which handles key generation in step 6

entries.symptom: Pipeline fails requiring --compare-manifest but no baseline manifest exists
entries.root_cause: First run has no baseline to compare against
entries.fix: Use --allow-missing-compare for first-run bootstrap only
entries.prevention: After first successful run, always save manifest as baseline for subsequent runs

entries.symptom: evaluation_quality_gate fails because evaluation_metric_path leaf does not match primary_metric
entries.root_cause: Request JSON declares primary_metric as one name but evaluation_metric_path points to a different metric
entries.fix: Ensure evaluation_metric_path terminal token matches primary_metric after normalization (e.g., pr_auc matches pr_auc)
entries.prevention: Use consistent metric naming. For publication-grade, primary_metric must be pr_auc.

entries.symptom: Covariate shift gate warns/fails due to high train-vs-test separability (AUC > 0.7)
entries.root_cause: Significant distribution drift between training and holdout data
entries.fix: Investigate feature distributions across splits; consider temporal re-splitting or feature removal for highly drifting features
entries.prevention: Use covariate_shift_gate.py early in pipeline. For temporal data, ensure splits respect temporal ordering.

entries.symptom: External validation gate fails because transport drop exceeds threshold
entries.root_cause: Model performance degrades significantly on external cohort compared to internal test
entries.fix: Investigate domain differences; consider domain adaptation, feature harmonization, or recalibration
entries.prevention: Include external cohort early in development cycle. Check distribution overlap before expecting good transport.

entries.symptom: Calibration gate fails because ECE exceeds threshold on internal or external cohort
entries.root_cause: Probability calibration not applied or inadequate for the model family
entries.fix: Enable calibration in training (--calibration-method isotonic or platt). For small datasets use platt.
entries.prevention: Always enable calibration. Check calibration plots. For tree-based models, isotonic often works better.

entries.symptom: Journal reviewers flag missing subgroup equity analysis; fairness metrics computed but never validated by any gate
entries.root_cause: Fairness metrics (equalized_odds_gap, disparate_impact_ratio) were computed in train_select_evaluate.py but no gate validated them against thresholds
entries.fix: Added fairness_equity_gate.py (gate #30) that validates equalized odds gap (<0.15), disparate impact ratio (>0.80/four-fifths rule), and per-subgroup PR-AUC minimums
entries.prevention: Any metric computed in training pipeline should have a corresponding gate that validates it against fail-closed thresholds

entries.symptom: Small dataset models pass all gates despite EPV < 10; reviewers ask for sample size justification
entries.root_cause: EPV was computed and reported but never hard-gated. Riley et al. 2019/2025 criteria not enforced
entries.fix: Added sample_size_gate.py that enforces EPV >= 10 (fail), >= 20 (warn), min 100 events, shrinkage factor >= 0.9
entries.prevention: All quantitative adequacy metrics should have corresponding fail-closed gates, not just informational reporting

entries.symptom: TRIPOD+AI 2024 has 27 items but gate only validated 11; reviewers reject for incomplete adherence
entries.root_cause: reporting_bias_gate.py only included original subset of TRIPOD+AI items, missing 2024 additions
entries.fix: Added 6 new required items: study_design_and_data_source_described, outcome_prevalence_or_event_rate_reported, calibration_metrics_reported, fairness_assessment_reported, model_availability_or_code_sharing_reported, data_availability_statement_provided
entries.prevention: Track guideline version updates (TRIPOD+AI BMJ 2024;385:e078378) and update gate validation items accordingly

entries.symptom: Top journals (JAMA, Lancet) require IDI alongside NRI for model comparison; only NRI was available
entries.root_cause: Integrated Discrimination Improvement (Pencina et al. 2008) not implemented despite NRI being present
entries.fix: Added _integrated_discrimination_improvement() function computing IDI, discrimination slope, and relative IDI vs both prevalence and logistic baselines
entries.prevention: When implementing one model comparison metric (NRI), also implement its companion metrics (IDI, DeLong) for completeness

entries.symptom: Nature Medicine and Lancet DH strongly recommend SHAP-based feature explanations; only permutation importance available
entries.root_cause: SHAP library not integrated; only model-agnostic permutation importance implemented
entries.fix: Not yet resolved. SHAP is optional dependency. Consider adding optional SHAP computation when shap package is installed.
entries.prevention: Track journal requirements for interpretability. SHAP is becoming standard for tree-based and linear models.

entries.symptom: Reviewers ask whether missingness is MCAR/MAR/MNAR; framework validates policy but not mechanism
entries.root_cause: missingness_policy_gate enforces imputation strategy compliance but does not test the missing data mechanism (Little's MCAR test)
entries.fix: Not yet resolved. Consider adding optional Little's MCAR test and MNAR sensitivity analysis.
entries.prevention: Distinguish between policy enforcement (is the right method used?) and mechanism validation (is the assumption justified?)

entries.symptom: PROBAST+AI was updated in BMJ March 2025 with expanded signalling questions; current gate uses 2019 version
entries.root_cause: PROBAST+AI update (Moons et al. BMJ 2025) expanded from original to 16 development + 18 evaluation signalling questions
entries.fix: Not yet resolved. Current 6-item PROBAST check covers core domains but should be expanded to match 2025 signalling questions.
entries.prevention: Monitor BMJ/EQUATOR Network for guideline updates annually

entries.symptom: JAMA Network Open 2025 showed ICD codes can leak same-admission outcomes (label leakage)
entries.root_cause: Diagnostic codes assigned during same hospitalization can encode the outcome being predicted
entries.fix: definition_variable_guard.py already blocks disease-defining variables; feature_lineage_gate.py blocks lineage leakage. But same-admission ICD code leakage is a subtler pattern that requires temporal awareness.
entries.prevention: When using ICD/diagnosis codes as predictors, verify they were assigned BEFORE the prediction time point (t_index), not during the same encounter.

entries.symptom: batch_journal_review.py fails with JSON parse error or missing required fields in manifest
entries.root_cause: Batch manifest file does not conform to batch_manifest.v1 contract (missing 'projects' array or invalid project entries)
entries.fix: Validate manifest against references/batch-manifest.example.json template. Each project entry requires 'id' and 'path' fields.
entries.prevention: Use batch-manifest.example.json as template. Validate JSON before running batch review.

entries.symptom: Batch review reports 'Project directory not found' for one or more projects in manifest
entries.root_cause: Project path in manifest does not exist on disk or is not accessible
entries.fix: Verify all project paths in manifest exist and are absolute paths. Use expanduser() for ~ paths.
entries.prevention: Validate project paths before running batch review. Use absolute paths in manifest.

agent_instructions.on_new_error: When encountering a new error not in this database: 1) Diagnose root cause 2) Apply fix 3) Verify fix works 4) Append new entry to this file with next sequential ID 5) Commit with message 'knowledge-base: add ERR-NNN <brief description>'
agent_instructions.on_known_error: When encountering a known error: 1) Look up the entry 2) Apply the documented fix 3) If fix doesn't work, update the entry with additional context 4) If the error has a new variant, add a sub-entry
agent_instructions.id_format: ERR-NNN where NNN is zero-padded sequential number
